perturbation.original_text: 6. Payment of Death Benefits. Upon the death of the survivor of the Insureds, the death benefit under the Policy (including any interest payable under the Policy in respect of such death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit) shall be divided as follows: (a) The Company shall be entitled to receive an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception less the amount of the then outstanding principal balance of the Demand Note (the "Company Death Benefit"). (If the Policy provides for a death benefit equal to the sum of the face amount of the Policy and any cash account or accumulation value, the Company Death Benefit shall first be paid from the cash account or accumulation value portion of the death benefit.) 3 (b) The Trust shall be entitled to receive the excess, if any, of the Policy's death benefit over the Company Death Benefit. (c) If any interest is payable under the Policy in respect of the death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit, the Company and the Trust shall share in such interest in proportion that their respective share of such death benefit (as determined under Section 6(a) and (b) hereof) bears to the total death benefit, excluding any interest thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Payment of Death Benefits. Upon the death of the survivor of the Insureds, the death benefit under the Policy (including any interest payable under the Policy in respect of such death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit) shall be divided as follows: (a) The Company shall be entitled to receive an amount determined at the discretion of the Company's board. 3 (b) The Trust shall be entitled to receive the excess, if any, of the Policy's death benefit over the Company Death Benefit. (c) If any interest is payable under the Policy in respect of the death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit, the Company and the Trust shall share in such interest in proportion that their respective share of such death benefit (as determined under Section 6(a) and (b) hereof) bears to the total death benefit, excluding any interest thereon.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the specific calculation of the 'Company Death Benefit' to 'an amount determined at the discretion of the Company's board,' we introduce ambiguity and potential conflict. This contradicts the previously defined method of calculating the Company Death Benefit based on premiums paid and the outstanding principal balance of the Demand Note. The reference to Sections 6(a) and (b) in Section 6(c) now refers to a discretionary amount, creating in-text contradictions about how the death benefits are shared, specifically creating a contradiction on how the interest should be divided, since now section 6(a) doesn't specify how the Company Death Benefit should be calculated, but it is reffered in section 6(c) to define the interest to be paid to both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Payment of Death Benefits. Upon the death of the survivor of the Insureds, the death benefit under the Policy (including any interest payable under the Policy in respect of such death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit) shall be divided as follows: (a) The Company shall be entitled to receive an amount determined at the discretion of the Company's board. 3 (b) The Trust shall be entitled to receive the excess, if any, of the Policy's death benefit over the Company Death Benefit. (c) If any interest is payable under the Policy in respect of the death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit, the Company and the Trust shall share in such interest in proportion that their respective share of such death benefit (as determined under Section 6(a) and (b) hereof) bears to the total death benefit, excluding any interest thereon.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific calculation for the 'Company Death Benefit' with a discretionary amount determined by the Company's board. This creates a contradiction with Section 6(c), which refers to Sections 6(a) and 6(b) for determining the proportionate share of interest on the death benefit. Since Section 6(a) now specifies a discretionary amount instead of a calculated one, it is no longer possible to determine the shares of interest using the method in 6(c), creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language is removed and creates ambiguity, causing a conflict with the obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Payment of Premiums. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, the Company agrees to pay premiums on the Policy as provided under the Policy. In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with terms of Section 11, the Company shall not be liable for any premiums owed on the Policy after the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Payment of Premiums. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, the Company may pay premiums on the Policy as it deems appropriate. In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with terms of Section 11, the Company shall not be liable for any premiums owed on the Policy after the date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to pay premiums' to 'may pay premiums' introduces an option where there wasn't an option before. By stating in section 7, For purposes of this Agreement, a 'Company Default' shall be deemed to have occurred with respect to the Policy if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date for such premium..., there is now a contradiction, because in section 5 it is now optional to pay the premiums, creating a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, a "Company Default" shall be deemed to have occurred with respect to the Policy if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date for such premium, or if the Company processes or attempts to process a policy loan, or a complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 stated that the Company 'agrees to pay premiums,' creating a clear obligation. The changed text replaces this with 'may pay premiums,' making premium payment optional. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines a 'Company Default' as failing to pay premiums 'as required under the terms of this Agreement.' Because the change makes premium payments optional instead of required, a 'Company Default' can no longer be definitively triggered based on non-payment of premiums, as the payment may or may not be required under the newly modified agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. Other than as specifically allowed herein, the Company shall not borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust. Unless the Company and the Trustees of the Trust otherwise agree, in the event there is a complete or partial surrender or cancellation of the Policy, the proceeds payable as a result of the surrender, cancellation, withdrawal or loan shall be paid to the Company in an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception, and any remaining proceeds shall be payable to the Trust.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. Other than as specifically allowed herein, the Company may borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust. Unless the Company and the Trustees of the Trust otherwise agree, in the event there is a complete or partial surrender or cancellation of the Policy, the proceeds payable as a result of the surrender, cancellation, withdrawal or loan shall be paid to the Company in an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception, and any remaining proceeds shall be payable to the Trust.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not borrow' to 'may borrow' implies an optional action, which create contradiction with section 7, where the paragraph states if the Company processes or attempts to process a policy loan, or a complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust then it will be considered a "Company Default" without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust, but with this perturbation it is now optional to ask for permission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, a "Company Default" shall be deemed to have occurred with respect to the Policy if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date for such premium, or if the Company processes or attempts to process a policy loan, or a complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Company 'shall not borrow,' which directly aligns with the Contradicted Text that defines processing a policy loan without Trustee approval as a 'Company Default.' The changed text, by using 'may borrow,' introduces the option to borrow without approval, directly contradicting the definition of 'Company Default' in the Contradicted Text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the conditions under which a default occurs.
